_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how pictures and words work together to help us understand stories and information texts.

_source.w: We are learning how pictures and words work together to help us understand stories and information texts.
y: Pictures and words each tell us something different — together they tell us more.
Understanding how images and words combine helps us get the full meaning of a text.
This skill helps us with all kinds of texts, from picture books to websites.
eg: We point to the words on a page and then to the picture, noticing how they connect.
We look at a page with both words and a picture and talk about what each one tells us.
We cover the words and guess the story from just the pictures, then check with the words.
We cover the pictures and try to understand the story from just the words.
We notice when a picture adds extra information that the words do not say.
We talk about how illustrations help us understand what characters look like or feel.
